title: UPI Intent Payment Handler — Schema Bundle
description: JSON Schema definitions for the UPI Intent payment handler for ACP (Agentic Commerce Protocol). Enables UPI payments via deep links on mobile and QR codes on desktop. This schema is PSP-agnostic; Razorpay is the reference implementation (handler name: com.razorpay.upi_intent).

BusinessConfig.description: Configuration for the UPI Intent handler provided by the business. Declared in the business's ACP handler configuration.

key_id.description: PSP API key identifier for this business account.

merchant_vpa.description: Merchant UPI Virtual Payment Address (VPA) assigned by the acquiring bank or PSP during merchant onboarding (e.g., storename@icici). This is an NPCI-standard identifier and is not specific to any one PSP. Used as the payee address in all UPI intent URIs generated for this business.

environment.description: PSP API environment. Use 'sandbox' for test mode, 'production' for live mode.

PlatformConfig.description: Platform-level configuration for the UPI Intent handler. Declared in the platform's ACP handler configuration.

environment.description: PSP API environment. Must match the business config environment.

upi_apps.description: Ordered list of UPI app identifiers the platform supports for deep-link intent (e.g., 'phonepe', 'gpay', 'paytm', 'bhim'). Platform presents these in order for mobile intent flow.

PaymentMethodUPIIntent.description: UPI Intent payment method. On mobile, the platform generates a UPI deep-link and opens the user's UPI app. On desktop, a QR code is rendered for the user to scan. No pre-authorization or stored credential is required — each transaction is user-initiated.

intent_uri.description: NPCI-compliant UPI intent URI (upi:// scheme) for mobile deep-link execution. Generated by the platform using merchant_vpa and transaction details.

transaction_reference.description: Transaction reference ID (max 35 alphanumeric characters per NPCI UPI Linking Specification). Used to correlate the UPI payment with the ACP checkout session.

qr_code_data.description: Optional base64-encoded PNG QR code image for desktop rendering. Encodes the same intent_uri for scanning with any UPI app. Platforms MAY omit this field and instead derive the QR code locally from intent_uri — the QR payload is always identical to the intent_uri value.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this intent URI and QR code expire. Platforms SHOULD set a 15-minute expiry per NPCI best practice.

RiskSignal.description: Fraud detection signal. UPI Intent payments do not carry card-testing signals — platforms SHOULD submit an empty risk_signals array.

action.description: Recommended action based on risk assessment

Allowance.description: Constraints on how the UPI Intent payment can be used for a single transaction (amount limit, expiration, merchant). Identical shape to the base ACP Allowance.

reason.description: Usage pattern for this allowance; currently only one_time is supported

max_amount.description: Maximum charge amount in minor units (paise for INR). Must match the amount encoded in the intent_uri.

currency.description: ISO-4217 three-letter lowercase currency code. For UPI Intent, always 'inr'.

checkout_session_id.description: Identifier of the ACP checkout session this payment is for. SHOULD be used as transaction_reference in the intent URI.

merchant_id.description: Unique identifier for the merchant authorized to collect this payment

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this allowance expires. SHOULD match PaymentMethodUPIIntent.expires_at.

DelegatePaymentRequest.description: Request to initiate a UPI Intent payment. The platform generates a UPI intent URI, optionally derives a QR code from it, presents both to the user, and monitors for payment completion before submitting this request. Polling cadence and retry limits are operational concerns — see NPCI UPI Linking Specification for implementer guidance.

payment_method.description: The UPI Intent payment method with intent URI and QR code

allowance.description: Constraints on how this payment can be used for this transaction

billing_address.description: Billing address associated with the payment. Optional for UPI Intent — UPI uses VPA-based addressing with no billing address requirement.

risk_signals.description: Risk assessment signals from the platform's fraud detection system. UPI Intent payments do not carry card-testing signals — platforms SHOULD submit an empty array.

DelegatePaymentResponse.description: Response confirming the UPI Intent payment was accepted. PSP-specific confirmation values (e.g., payment_id, upi_txn_id) are returned in metadata for merchant reconciliation.

id.description: Unique identifier for this delegated payment record

created.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this delegated payment record was created

metadata.description: Metadata echoed from the request plus system-added fields. MAY include PSP-specific confirmation values such as payment_id and upi_txn_id for merchant reconciliation.

Error.description: Error response for UPI Intent delegated payment requests

code.description: Specific error code for programmatic handling

supported_versions.description: List of API versions supported by the server, ordered by preference (newest first). Only included in version-related errors.

example.message: UPI intent URI has expired. Generate a new intent URI and QR code before retrying.
